summary: The window carries strong reveal content, but chapter 14 repeats chapter 13's travel-and-reorientation shape and chapter 16 both skips a visible state handoff and replays Kem's decision to keep going, which softens the run-up to the Scour.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3765 establishes Kem at twenty-five at the end of the last anchored Kem chapter; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4020 leaves the group in the farmhouse in full daylight; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4031 reopens before sunrise in an abandoned waystation; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4055 suddenly places Kem at eighteen with no bridge for the elapsed travel or off-page spending.
findings.problem: The chapter 15 to 16 handoff asks the reader to infer too much at once: at least one overnight, a relocation from farmhouse to waystation, and seven missing levels from Kem's Ledger. Because the bridge is absent, Dren's departure starts on top of avoidable orientation work instead of cleanly inheriting the prior chapter's pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add a brief opening bridge in chapter 16 that fixes the elapsed time, names the move from the farmhouse, and accounts for the incidental spending or survival costs that took Kem from twenty-five to eighteen.
findings.acceptance_test: By the time chapter 16 states Kem's current level, the reader can tell how much time has passed since Sable left, why the group is at the waystation, and what on-page or explicitly summarized costs produced Kem's new number.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Chapter 16 owns the problem because it introduces the new place, the new dawn, and the new level state without supplying the boundary handoff.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3354 already tells the reader the route journal contains two hands and that Vel handles it with unusual care; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3741 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3745 restage the same clue package one chapter later before chapter 16 pays it off.
findings.problem: Back-to-back Kem chapters repeat the same journal inference instead of trusting the reader to carry it forward. The second pass slows chapter 14's campsite material because it feels like a recap of a clue that is still fresh from chapter 13.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either trim the clueing in chapter 13 or let chapter 14 advance it with only the genuinely new realization, rather than re-explaining both the two handwritings and Vel's tenderness toward the journal.
findings.acceptance_test: Reading chapters 13 and 14 consecutively, the journal clue lands once, then progresses; chapter 14 should add new leverage rather than reheating the same suspicion.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Chapter 14 owns the repetition because the later chapter repeats information that chapter 13 has already made legible.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3284, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3294, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3296 open chapter 13 with degraded-road description, Dren's bad arm, and Hoarded extraction signage before the confrontation beat; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3543, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3553, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3563 reopen chapter 14 with the same road decay, level arithmetic, silence, and infrastructure notices before the rescue scene starts.
findings.problem: Two consecutive Kem chapters arrive through nearly the same doorway: bad road, depleted Margins, current Ledger count, Dren's injury, then the actual chapter event. That repeated start makes chapter 14 feel like chapter 13 restarting instead of a fresh movement after the fracture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or compress the second travel-and-ledger reorientation so chapter 14 reaches the sinking settlement sooner, or bake the necessary status update into the rescue setup rather than dedicating another opening page to the same on-road inventory.
findings.acceptance_test: The first page of chapter 14 should feel structurally distinct from chapter 13's first page; on a consecutive read, the rescue arrives before the reader has sat through another full road/number/silence reset.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Chapter 14 owns the issue because it is the later chapter that repeats an opening shape chapter 13 has just used.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3753, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3761, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3769 already land Kem's decision that he will not walk away; after chapter 15 escalates the Scour threat, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4129, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4137, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4243, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4251 stop the road again so he can refuse more steps until Vel tells the truth and then agree to keep walking.
findings.problem: The confession in chapter 16 is necessary, but the surrounding scene dynamic repeats a beat the window has already spent: Kem halts movement, truth comes out, Kem recommits to the southward march. Because chapter 14 has already made his keep-going choice feel interiorly settled, chapter 16's version reads less like escalation than like a second pass over the same leverage question.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either leave chapter 14's ending more provisional so chapter 16 earns the actual recommitment, or keep chapter 16's confession but change the consequence so the post-confession state is materially different from 'we are still walking south.'
findings.acceptance_test: After chapter 16, the partnership or plan has changed in a concrete way that chapter 14 did not already deliver; the scene should not read as another iteration of pause, reveal, resume.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Chapter 16 owns the redundancy because the later chapter replays the continue-or-stop dynamic after chapter 14 has already landed Kem's choice and chapter 15 has already supplied fresh external stakes.
